satire_attempt: Engineering Director Issues Slack Decree: 'No More Eulogies For Deprecated Services.' Sources confirm the director, who personally wrote the v3 routing service in 2022, has cited 'Krishna's therefore-clause' as authority for shutting down a memorial thread. Critics note the director also disabled the company's #deletions-and-deprecations channel last year, citing 'tasmād evaṁ viditvā.' The director's own LinkedIn celebrates the 47,000-line v3 codebase as 'a foundational contribution to logistics infrastructure.'
verdict_reason: The satire only lands if you ignore the verse's counter-example, which explicitly disqualifies performative non-grief: 'Performative non-grief over a real spec-loss is its own dishonesty.' The verse precisely says: do not grieve what was implementation when the spec survives. It does not say all grief is misplaced; it identifies the mature engineer's grief as information pointing at unwritten specs. The implication ('If yes, drop the eulogy and resume work. If no, the urgent task is... writing down the spec') makes both directions operational. To make the satire land, you would have to disagree with the meta-claim that some deprecations are at the cloth layer and some are at the spec layer — a real engineering distinction you can argue with rather than mock. PASS.
